reviewed_by: independent row QA workers plus independent final visual QA worker

directions.observed: Clear upward gaze with frontal grounded stance.
directions.reason: Both blue eyes aim above center, brows lift, chin rises slightly, and nose remains centered; hard-gate pose matches the approved 000 anchor.
Eyes remain elevated while pupils, nose, and head begin moving screen-right; feet and baseline remain registered to 000.
Elevated eyes combine with a clearer screen-right nose and head yaw; hair, shoulders, torso, and occlusion advance naturally.
Nose and visible eye lead strongly screen-right while retaining an upward pitch; body scale, lower anchor, and feet remain stable.
Nose tip, visible eye, facial contour, head yaw, and shoulder plane all point toward the image-right edge; hard-gate pose matches the approved 090 anchor.
Rightward nose and profile are preserved while the eye, eyelid, brow, and chin move downward; no scale or registration jump from 090.
Top-of-head visibility and lowered eyes establish down while the face and nose remain screen-right; identity and grounded lower body stay intact.

directions.observed: Near-down pose retaining a subtle rightward cue.
directions.reason: Eyes and tucked chin read strongly downward, with remaining rightward facial asymmetry completing the ordered approach toward 180.
Both blue eyes sit low, eyelids and brows angle down, chin tucks, and extra top-of-hair surface is visible; the grounded frontal body matches the approved 180 hard gate.
Lowered eyes and tucked chin preserve the downward axis while nose, facial plane, and head yaw begin moving screen-left; feet and baseline remain fixed.
Eye line and chin remain lowered while the nose and visible facial contour lead farther screen-left; hair, shoulders, and torso advance gradually without identity drift.
The nose and visible eye point strongly toward the image-left edge while lowered eyelids and head pitch retain a clear downward component; scale and lower-body registration stay stable.
Nose tip, visible eye, facial contour, head yaw, and shoulder plane all face the image-left edge, opposing the approved 090 pose and matching the approved 270 hard gate.

directions.observed: Left profile beginning to pitch upward.
directions.reason: The nose and facial plane remain screen-left while the eye, brow, chin, and head angle rise; upright legs and planted feet rule out whole-sprite rotation.
Elevated blue eyes and lifted chin establish up while nose placement and facial asymmetry remain screen-left; hair and shoulders follow continuously.

directions.observed: Near-up pose retaining a subtle leftward cue.
directions.reason: Both eyes aim high and the chin lifts, while remaining screen-left nose and cheek asymmetry complete the ordered approach to 000 without reversal.

continuity.reason: All adjacent pairs progress smoothly from up through screen-right toward down-right using ordered eye, eyelid, brow, nose, head, hair, and shoulder changes; scale, baseline, feet, pixel identity, and lower-body registration remain stable with no clipping, edge contact, overlap, whole-sprite rotation, replacement eyes, snap, reversal, or wrong-quadrant pose.

All row-10 neighbors progress smoothly from down through screen-left to up-left with stable pixel identity, scale, baseline, feet, eye construction, and lower-body anchor; 157.5->180 closes from slight down-right to centered down in one step, and 337.5->000 closes from slight up-left to centered up in one step, with no clipping, edge contact, overlap, snap, whole-sprite rotation, wrong quadrant, or reversal.

review_warnings: 090->112.5 metric diff/center shift accepted: coherent pitch-down with grounded feet.
157.5->180 metric diff/center shift accepted: smooth down-right to centered-down transition with stable baseline.
337.5->000 metric diff/center shift/area ratio accepted: natural three-quarter up-left to frontal-up opening without visible pop or reversal.
